methodology: Greedy per-pick assignment using the independent model's 727-prospect board (kalshi-anchored + PFF tape) × each team's roster_needs, gm_affinity, scheme premium, and documented pre-draft visits. Diminishing-returns: each additional pick at the same position halves the need weight. Reach + over-value penalties applied per round band.

picks.reasoning: LV opens the draft with Fernando Mendoza  -  the only pick in the 2026 class with near-unanimous consensus and market pricing. Everything after this gets murkier. Had a documented pre-draft visit with LV. Jordyn Tyson (#7, WR) was the live alternate  -  credible enough that a re-sim could flip this slot.

NYJ takes Arvell Reese on value  -  no premium LB need, but the grade gap at this slot made the call straightforward. Fits the 3-4 multiple (Glenn) scheme profile the staff has built around; had a documented pre-draft visit with NYJ. This was a close call over Sonny Styles (LB, ranked #4); the model picked Arvell Reese on marginal fit advantage, not grade separation. At this slot, this is a lean, not a certainty  -  every pick after #1 has credible alternates inside the same grade band.

ARI has a critical OT need  -  free agency didn't close it, and Kadyn Proctor is the cleanest on-board solution at 3. Fits the McVay tree (LaFleur) scheme profile the staff has built around; had a documented pre-draft visit with ARI. This was a close call over Max Iheanachor (OT, ranked #31); the model picked Kadyn Proctor on marginal fit advantage, not grade separation. At this slot, this is a lean, not a certainty  -  every pick after #1 has credible alternates inside the same grade band.
